title: cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis
intro: Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis, also known as Dinghu chestnut oak, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the family Fagaceae and is native to China. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially exhibiting anti-cancer properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, this plant is often used to prepare teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in the form of extracts or capsules, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
